purpose: MCP server that reads manifest/tools.json at startup and exposes each globally-scoped tool as a discrete Claude Code MCP tool, with args, cwd, and stdin inputs and a 30-second execution timeout.
called_by: Claude Code (spawns as MCP server via node)
calls_out_to: Any binary listed in manifest/tools.json with scope=global
invariants: Only manifest entries with scope=global are exposed as MCP tools
Denylist entries are always excluded regardless of manifest scope
Tool names are sanitized to [a-zA-Z0-9_-] before registration
All tool executions have a 30-second SIGTERM timeout
Both stdout and stderr are captured and returned in the tool response
gotchas: SKIP set currently excludes 'pillow' — it is a Python library injected into ipython, not a standalone CLI
BINARY_OVERRIDES maps 'gnu-time' to /usr/bin/time because the manifest binary 'time' resolves to the shell builtin
ARG_PREFIX prepends ['compose'] for 'docker-compose' so it is invoked as 'docker compose <args>'
The denylist file is loaded at startup; errors loading it result in an empty denylist (silent fail)
Adding tools to the manifest does not require restarting the server process — but Claude Code must start a new session to pick up new tool definitions

key_functions.name: sanitizeName
key_functions.summary: Converts a manifest tool name to a valid MCP tool name by replacing disallowed characters

key_functions.name: buildDescription
key_functions.summary: Constructs a tool description from manifest notes and install_method fields

key_functions.name: run
key_functions.summary: Spawns a child process, collects stdout/stderr, enforces 30s timeout, and resolves with combined text output
